french-revolution-1789.label: French Revolution
french-revolution-1789.description: Enlightenment ideals erupt as revolution, shaking Europe's old order. The Viennese court is alarmed, but revolutionary ideas can no longer be stopped.

napoleon-holy-roman-empire-1806.label: Dissolution of the Holy Roman Empire
napoleon-holy-roman-empire-1806.description: Napoleon forces Franz II to give up the title of 'Holy Roman Emperor'; the thousand-year empire is nominally dissolved, and Austria is renamed the 'Austrian Empire'.

congress-of-vienna-1815.label: Congress of Vienna
congress-of-vienna-1815.description: After the Napoleonic Wars, Europe's powers redraw the political map in Vienna. The city becomes Europe's diplomatic centre, prosperous as never before. 'The Congress dances, but it does not advance.'

revolution-1848.label: Revolutions of 1848
revolution-1848.description: A wave of revolution sweeps Europe; Viennese citizens and students take to the streets, Metternich flees, and the Habsburg court totters. Repression eventually restores order, but reform has taken root.

ausgleich-1867.label: Austro-Hungarian Compromise
ausgleich-1867.description: Austria and Hungary reach the Compromise, forming the dual monarchy of 'Austria-Hungary'. Vienna enters the peak of Ringstraße construction and rapid modernisation.

secession-founded-1897.label: Founding of the Vienna Secession
secession-founded-1897.description: Klimt and other artists break from the conservative academy to found the 'Secession', proclaiming artistic freedom. The same year, the antisemitic politician Karl Lueger is elected mayor — the city's light and shadow side by side.

turn-of-century-1900.label: Turn of the Century
turn-of-century-1900.description: Freud publishes The Interpretation of Dreams; Vienna is in its cultural golden age — Secession, psychoanalysis, new architecture and a literary revolution erupting at once, the old empire's sunset especially brilliant.

wwi-1914.label: Outbreak of the First World War
wwi-1914.description: After the assassination in Sarajevo, Austria-Hungary declares war on Serbia, igniting the World War. Vienna's golden age halts abruptly — what Zweig called the end of 'the world of yesterday'.

republic-austria-1918.label: Collapse of the Habsburg Empire
republic-austria-1918.description: WWI ends, the 650-year Habsburg dynasty falls, and the Republic of Austria is founded. Vienna shrinks from capital of a 52-million empire to the oversized metropolis of a 1.8-million state.

anschluss-1938.label: Anschluss (Annexation of Austria)
anschluss-1938.description: In March 1938 Nazi Germany annexes Austria. Viennese intellectuals — Freud, Zweig, Wittgenstein — flee into exile, and Vienna's cultural golden age comes to a definitive close.
